food_subgroup: Hot and cold beverage bundle — kopitiam-style coffee and tea drinks

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a multi-item kopitiam drinks bundle. FOODON_03301099 (beverage food product) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00FL (Coffee and coffee substitutes) is used as the closest parent. The bundle may include tea and other kopitiam beverages; no exact FoodEx2 code exists for a mixed kopitiam drinks bundle.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps to a commercial multi-drink kopitiam bundle. Left null.

preparation_method: mixed with condensed milk or evaporated milk

serving.natural_language_portion: One bundle consists of four standard kopitiam-style drinks (approximately 200 g each), totalling approximately 800 g, as served at Toast Box Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Kopi (Coffee with condensed milk, Singapore-style) — HPB Singapore Foodo / USDA FoodData Central reference for coffee with milk and sugar
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: The bundle is a commercial promotional item (4 drinks at 10% off) with no single nutrient entry in any approved database. Nutrients are estimated as a weighted average of typical kopitiam beverages: kopi (coffee with condensed milk and sugar) and teh (tea with condensed milk and sugar), using HPB Singapore Foodo data for kopi and teh, and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for brewed coffee and tea components. Values represent a per-100 g average across the four-drink bundle assuming a typical kopitiam drink composition. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sweetened coffee and tea beverages with condensed milk — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for sweetened milk-based beverages and condensed milk
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Toast Box kopi or teh bundle. GI of 55 is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database entries for sweetened condensed-milk beverages and coffee with sugar and milk, which cluster in the GI 50–60 range. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 70.4) / 100 ≈ 39.0, where carbohydrate per 800 g serving = 8.8 × 8 = 70.4 g.

hypertension.guidance: Each bundle provides approximately 224 mg sodium across four drinks; individuals with hypertension should monitor total daily sodium intake and be aware that caffeine in kopi may transiently raise blood pressure, so limiting to one or two cups per day is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High added sugar from condensed milk
type2_diabetes.guidance: Kopitiam-style drinks made with condensed milk and sugar carry a low-to-medium GI (approximately 55) and a high glycemic load per bundle (~39), making them unsuitable for unrestricted consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; substituting with kopi-o kosong (black coffee, no sugar) or teh-o kosong is strongly recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from condensed milk and added sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat contributed by condensed milk (~0.9 g per 100 g) and the high sugar load across four drinks may contribute to dyslipidaemia and elevated triglycerides; individuals with cardiovascular disease should choose unsweetened or reduced-sugar kopitiam beverage options.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Toast Box 4x Drinks Bundle is a promotional kopitiam beverage set sold at Toast Box outlets in Singapore, such as the Funan Mall branch. The bundle comprises four standard kopitiam-style drinks — typically a combination of kopi (Nanyang-style coffee brewed from Robusta beans roasted with butter and sugar, mixed with condensed milk), teh (Ceylon tea with condensed milk and sugar), or their variants such as kopi-o (black coffee with sugar), teh-o (black tea with sugar), kopi-c (coffee with evaporated milk), or teh-c (tea with evaporated milk). The drinks are served hot or iced depending on customer preference. Nutritionally, each drink is a sweetened milk-based beverage with moderate energy (~52 kcal per 100 g), low protein (~1.2 g), low fat (~1.4 g, predominantly saturated from condensed milk), and moderate carbohydrate (~8.8 g, mostly added sugar). The bundle's glycemic load is high (~39 per bundle) due to the cumulative sugar from condensed milk across four servings, making it a concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. Sodium is moderate (~28 mg per 100 g). The bundle is halal-certified and vegetarian-friendly but not vegan due to dairy content. It is a culturally significant offering in Singapore's kopitiam heritage, representing the traditional Nanyang coffee culture brought by Hainanese immigrants in the early 20th century. Toast Box is a modern kopitiam chain that preserves this heritage in a contemporary café setting.
common_user_queries: What is in the Toast Box 4 drinks bundle?
How many calories in Toast Box kopi bundle?
Is Toast Box drinks bundle halal?
Is kopi bundle suitable for diabetics?
What drinks are in Toast Box bundle?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source nutrient entry exists for a Toast Box 4x Drinks Bundle as a composite commercial product. Nutrient values are estimated from HPB Singapore Foodo and USDA FoodData Central data for individual kopitiam beverages (kopi and teh with condensed milk). The GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened milk-based beverages. Confidence is medium because the estimation is methodologically sound but not based on a direct laboratory analysis of this specific bundle.
notes: 1. This record represents a promotional bundle item (10% off, 4 drinks) sold on field survey data by Toast Box - Funan. The canonical food name has been normalised to 'Toast Box 4x Drinks Bundle', removing the promotional pricing prefix '(10% OFF)' as it is not part of the food identity. 2. Nutrient values are per-100 g estimates based on a weighted average of typical kopitiam beverages (kopi and teh with condensed milk and sugar) using HPB Singapore Foodo and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data. 3. Serving size is estimated at 800 g (4 × ~200 g cups). 4. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as brewed coffee and tea beverages with condensed milk contain no dietary fibre per HPB and USDA sources. 5. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as kopitiam beverages contain no vitamin C. 6. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero by product formulation; condensed milk and Robusta coffee contain negligible trans fats. 7. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB or USDA source data for these beverage types. 8. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for kopitiam beverages. 9. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for kopitiam beverages. 10. GI set to 55 (low) as an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for sweetened condensed-milk beverages; glycemic_load_per_serving of 39.0 calculated for the full 800 g bundle. 11. The bundle composition may vary by customer order (e.g., all kopi, all teh, or mixed); nutrient values assume a typical mixed kopi/teh composition. Follow-up: obtain direct nutritional data from Toast Box if available.
